summary: Hotz proposes 'Wireheading City,' a radical solution to homelessness: a 5-mile diameter city in Nevada where people can opt out of society entirely. Residents receive free food, shelter, entertainment, and unlimited access to drugs including alcohol, weed, meth, and opioids. The city functions as a voluntary alternative to both homelessness and prison, with strict border controls to prevent drug export. Hotz argues this would reduce prison populations, save money, and accommodate those who don't want to participate in the social contract they never signed.
key_points: The social contract is imposed without consent, and society should accommodate those who want to opt out
Wireheading City would provide UBI in the form of necessities (food, water, shelter, entertainment, drugs) rather than money
The city would be entirely voluntary with free entry and exit, distinguishing it from prison
Free unlimited drugs are the 'major draw' but export is strictly prohibited with aggressive border searches
Prison populations would drop significantly since many currently use prison as the only path to free food and shelter
money_quotes: There's a fine line that defines civilization.
You were never given the social contract to sign, it was just implied.
Not everyone wants to participate in society, and I think this is absolutely fine.
Wireheading City is the first city with UBI! Except, instead of money, we provide you with the basic necessities of life.
The free drugs are a major draw of Wireheading City.
It is not a prison, and you are free to leave at any time with just a 10 mile walk out.
Currently, prison is the only way to easily get free food and shelter in the US.
The real question is why are we not building this?
key_insight: Rather than forcing everyone into the same social contract, society should create opt-out zones where people can live with basic needs met while pursuing whatever self-destructive behaviors they choose.
